english: called sóg-ma (sog ma), in the third snyé-ma (snye ma)). — lo-ljaṅ-ba (lo ljang ba) having a green blade. — ljaṅ-bu (ljang bu) greenness, verdure (grass, foliage, shrubs), Lex.: साम्ब — ljaṅ-dmár (ljang dmar) greenish red; ljaṅ-sér (ljang ser) greenish yellow.
filth, dirt, dust, sweepings; lúd-pa (lud pa) ljan-ljiṅ (ljan ljin) maṅ (mang) a great deal of foul mucous expectoration Lt.
W. flat, plain, even; *ljab - ljáb-ba (ljab ljab ba) bor (bor)* lay or put it down flat; *ljab (ljab) có-te (co te) dug (dug)* sit down flat (on the ground)!

heaviness, weight, gser (gser) daṅ (dang) ljid-pa (ljid pa) mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) dgos (dgos) it must be weighed up with gold Glr.; de (de) daṅ (dang) ljid (ljid) mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) of equal weight, equal in weight Med.; ljid-can (ljid can), ljid-ldán (ljid ldan) heavy; ljid-ché-ba (ljid che ba) very heavy; ljid-méd (ljid med) light, not heavy; lus (lus) tams-cád-kyi (thams cad kyi) ljid (ljid) pab (phab) he sat down with the whole weight of his body Cs.; ljid-kyis (ljid kyis) nón-pa (non pa) pressing down by his(its) weight.
Cs. to enter, to penetrate, bló-la (blo la) one's mind, = to be perceived, understood; tson-ljén (tshon ljen) a die or colour penetrating and remaining fixed in cloth etc: Cf. źén-pa (zhen pa).

a large valley, principal or main valley; region, district, province Dzl.; ljoṅs (ljongs) daṅ (dang) yul-kór (yul 'khor) countries and provinces; ljoṅs (ljongs) čén-po (chen po) a large country; Ká-ba-can-gyi (kha ba can gyi) ljoṅs (ljongs) di ('di), gaṅs-can(-gyi) (gangs can (gyi)) ljoṅs (ljongs) Tibet, frq.; nágs-ljoṅs (nags ljongs) woody country; smán-ljoṅs (sman ljongs) a country of medicinal herbs Zam.; mú-geï (mu ge'i) ljoṅs (ljongs) a very poor country, starving country Mil.; ljoṅs-la (ljongs la) in the valley, in the plain; ljoṅs-mí-rnams (ljongs mi rnams) country-people Cs. — ljoṅs(-su) (ljongs (su)) rgyú-ba (rgyu ba) to rove about, ljoṅs (ljongs) sgyúr-ba (sgyur ba) the end of the estival fast of the monks (about the end of August), when they are permitted to rove about the whole district of their monastery.
a country of gods, paradise; ljon - śiṅ (ljon shing) a tree from paradise, or any large and beautiful tree; ljón-paï (ljon pa'i) nágs (nags) a beautiful forest.

Tar. 11, 14, but more frq. gzi-brjíd (gzi brjid), brightness, splendour, lustre, gen. of gods and saints, v. gzi (gzi); also dpal-brjid (dpal brjid) Lex.; brjíd-pa (brjid pa) to shine, glisten, glitter Cs., brjíd-kyis (brjid kyis) brjíd (brjid) shining with brightness Lex.
a making up, a compensation by barter, brjé-bo (brje bo) byéd-pa (byed pa) Glr., *brjé-bo (brje bo) gyáb-ce (rgyab ce)* W., to give an equal measure in bartering, e.g. of salt for barley.

(cf. rjód-pa (rjod pa)) sound; talking; speech, brjod (brjod) bdé-ba (bde ba) euphony; also well-sounding, agreeable speech; brjod (brjod) mi (mi) bdé-ba (bde ba) the contrary; also: *dha jo' (da lta) mi (mi) de (bde)* C. it is not meet now to speak about it; brjód-pa (brjod pa) speech, utterance; mṅon-brjód (mngon brjod) synonymy, explanation of words; Cs. also: 'a poetical term'; mčód-brjód (mchod brjod) praise, eulogy, Sch.: invocation of a deity; če-brjód (che brjod) Schr.(?), and čéd-du (ched du) brjód-pa (brjod pa), Tar. 140, 2 acc. to Schf.: preface, introduction, in C.: to approve, sanction, commend, Was. (270) in the title of a book: = उदान वर्ग w.e. Comp. brjód-bya (brjod bya) sbst., Zam. also brjód-pa (brjod pa), = वाच्य an attribute, predicate Lex. — brjod-méd (brjod med) 1. a speech not earnestly meant, empty words, mere talk. 2. Mil.: the unspeakable, the transcendental, identified by some with the Nirvana, by others not. — brjod-'dód (brjod 'dod) Tar. 210, 7: brjod-'dód-tsam (brjod 'dod tsam) acc. to Schf.: 'a mere supposition'; but in a passage in Mil. it seems to denote the (conceited) habit of constantly proposing one's own opinion, and so it might also be understood in Tar.
